query: Wang Yi China Foreign Minister visit South Korea 2026 postponed Taiwan dispute rescheduled July August 2026
answer:
I've completed my research. Here's the key finding:

## Bottom Line

The premise that Wang Yi's visit was **"rescheduled to July or August 2026" is NOT supported by available evidence.** The most recent concrete reporting (April 2026) shows the visit was stalled and unscheduled, with no confirmed dates.

**What actually happened:**
- A dispute arose over South Korea's electronic arrival card, which had labeled Taiwan as "China (Taiwan)." Taiwan protested (Dec 2025), so Seoul removed the departure/destination fields entirely (announced March 31, 2026; implemented April 10, 2026).
- China objected (April 14, 2026), reasserting the "one-China principle" and stating the "China (Taiwan)" label was "entirely natural."
- Korean media reported China put Wang Yi's proposed Seoul visit **on hold** in response.
- As of April 20, 2026, South Korea's MOFA said **no dates were finalized** and the two sides were "still communicating," downplaying the entry-card change as "a simple administrative and technical adjustment."
- In a related sign of friction, China reportedly blocked a visit by South Korea's intelligence chief (April 21, 2026).

**Important caveat:** My primary news database was unavailable (account limits), so I relied on web search. No source confirms a July/August 2026 rescheduling—either it didn't occur, or any such development postdates the accessible reporting. I've reported the trajectory (a stalled, indefinitely-delayed visit) rather than speculating on future outcomes, per my mandate.

The full synthesis, key facts, sources, and structured data points have been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Foreign ministry says no details finalized for Wang Yi's South Korea visit / China puts Wang Yi's Seoul visit on hold over Taiwan labeling dispute
sources.snippet: No specific dates finalized for Wang Yi's Seoul visit; China objected to removal of 'China (Taiwan)' label from e-arrival card; MOFA calls it a simple administrative adjustment.

sources.title: China blocks South Korean spy chief visit amid Taiwan tensions
sources.snippet: China refused a visit by South Korea's National Intelligence Service director amid Taiwan tensions in April 2026.

sources.title: S. Korea's revised e-arrival card eases exchanges between Taiwan, South Korea: MOFA
sources.snippet: South Korea revised its e-arrival card to no longer list Taiwan as part of China.

sources.title: Taipei protests after South Korea tourist arrival card lists Taiwan as 'China (Taiwan)'
sources.snippet: Taipei protested the 'China (Taiwan)' designation on South Korea's arrival card.

sources.title: Top Leader of the DPRK Kim Jong Un Meets with Wang Yi
sources.snippet: Wang Yi met Kim Jong Un in Pyongyang around April 10-12, 2026.

sources.title: Taiwan urges S Korea to correct name on platform
sources.snippet: In December 2025 Taiwan urged South Korea to correct the Taiwan designation on its arrival system.

key_facts: As of April 2026, no specific dates were finalized for Wang Yi's proposed visit to Seoul; the visit was effectively 'on hold' (Korea Times, Apr 20, 2026).
No available source confirms a rescheduling of the visit to July or August 2026; the 'rescheduled July/August 2026' premise is uncorroborated.
Root cause: a dispute over Taiwan being labeled 'China (Taiwan)' on South Korea's electronic arrival card, which Taipei protested in December 2025.
On March 31, 2026, South Korea announced it would eliminate the departure/destination fields from its e-arrival card; the disputed dropdown fields were removed on April 10, 2026.
On April 14, 2026, China's Foreign Ministry objected, stating 'Taiwan is part of China' and that labeling it 'China (Taiwan)' is 'entirely natural.'
Local Korean media reported China put Wang Yi's visit on hold after Seoul removed the Taiwan label.
Wang Yi visited Pyongyang and met Kim Jong Un around April 10-12, 2026; Seoul had hoped a Korea visit would follow shortly after.
On April 21, 2026, China reportedly refused/blocked a visit by South Korea's National Intelligence Service director amid Taiwan tensions (UPI).
South Korea's MOFA framed the entry-card change as 'a simple administrative and technical adjustment' unrelated to the visit scheduling.
South Korean FM Cho Hyun visited China in September 2025 at Wang Yi's invitation; the two maintained phone contact through late 2025 and 2026.

data_points.metric: Date South Korea announced elimination of departure/destination fields from e-arrival card
Date disputed dropdown fields removed from e-arrival card
Date China Foreign Ministry objected to Taiwan label removal
Date of Korea Times report stating no finalized dates for Wang Yi Seoul visit
Confirmed rescheduled dates for Wang Yi Seoul visit (July/August 2026)
